title: oenothera claviformis subsp. cruciformis
intro: Oenothera claviformis subsp. cruciformis, also known as Cross-Beaked Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its unique cruciform (cross-shaped) anthers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Oenothera claviformis subsp. cruciformis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
